title: James Evans (linguist)
description: English-Canadian Wesleyan Methodist missionary and amateur linguist
extract: James Evans (January 18, 1801 – November 23, 1846) was an English-Canadian Wesleyan Methodist missionary and amateur linguist. He is known for creating the syllabic writing system for Ojibwe and Cree, which was later adapted to other languages such as Inuktitut.
